content.boundary: Applies to school-choice/zoning rules and maintainer admissions exemptions across the whole primary/secondary network; explicitly excludes changing the age or grade-span of 6-/8-year gimnázium tracks.
content.change_lever: Network-level regulation of school-choice commuting, catchment-area design, and review of maintainer exemptions from anti-discrimination admissions provisions, independent of gimnázium track structure.
content.name: Structural desegregation without touching track age (choice and zoning reform)
content.system_problem: Research attributes Hungary's extreme between-school stratification primarily to structural factors (neighborhood segregation, admission policies, school-choice mechanisms) rather than the 6/8-year track alone, and towns with higher inter-school choice commuting show stronger inter-school inequalities.
